local.label: Local
local.description: bind to 127.0.0.1, only this machine can reach it

server.label: Server
server.description: bind to all interfaces, reachable from other devices

custom.label: Custom (advanced)
custom.short: I'll configure host, URL, and CORS myself
custom.description: advanced setup — choose where Cremind listens and how it's reached

advanced_fields.prompt: Where should Cremind listen for connections?
advanced_fields.hint: Use 127.0.0.1 to only allow this machine, or 0.0.0.0 to allow other devices / containers on the network.

advanced_fields.prompt: What URL will you use to open Cremind in a browser?
advanced_fields.hint: This is the address users type into their browser. Inside a container it's usually http://localhost:1515. On a server it might be http://my-box.lan:1515 or https://cremind.example.com.

advanced_fields.prompt: Which web origins should be allowed to talk to the API?
advanced_fields.hint: A comma-separated list of URLs the browser UI will be served from. Usually the same as the public URL above. Leave blank to use the public URL plus localhost variants.

advanced_fields.prompt: Which preset should pre-fill the Setup Wizard?
advanced_fields.hint: Presets fill in sensible defaults for the next setup screens — you can always edit any field afterwards. Pick `local` for a single-machine setup, `docker` for a docker-compose stack, or `server` for an external Postgres/Qdrant.

docker.label: Docker
docker.description: sandboxed container with a bundled storage stack (optional VNC desktop)
docker.hint: The agent runs inside a container. You can add a VNC desktop so it has its own GUI (observe at http://<host>:6080/vnc.html), or install the smaller headless image.

native.label: Native
native.description: Python venv at ~/.cremind/venv with embedded storage
native.hint: Simpler, but the agent shares your desktop and home directory.

docker_desktop.hint: Adds an XFCE desktop inside the container so you can watch the agent work at http://<host>:6080/vnc.html. Answer No to install the smaller headless image (cremind/cremind).

docker.label: Docker
docker.description_template: Cremind starts a {service} container alongside itself.
